claim: Coding agents without a validation framework skip testing—provide them an e2e testing command that guides them to test the app as users would, including frontend, backend, and database
actionable: Create or use an e2e testing skill with three parallel sub-agents: one for codebase structure, one for database schema, one for code review bugs
source_title: This One Command Makes Coding Agents Find All Their Mistakes (Use it Now)

claim: AI coding agents should only fix critical blockers during e2e testing and leave moderate/minor issues for human review, preventing over-fixing and keeping the feedback loop focused
actionable: Prompt the agent to fix only blocker issues; use the remaining issues output to guide next iteration rather than trying to fix everything
source_title: This One Command Makes Coding Agents Find All Their Mistakes (Use it Now)

claim: Use Neon's database branching feature when e2e testing with coding agents to keep test data isolated and avoid bloating production databases
actionable: Create a test branch of your database before running e2e tests; delete it after validation completes
source_title: This One Command Makes Coding Agents Find All Their Mistakes (Use it Now)

claim: Structure e2e testing output consistently (what was fixed, remaining issues, everything tested) so you can quickly review results and validate agent thoroughness
actionable: Require structured output format in your e2e testing prompt; include sections for fixed issues, remaining issues, and comprehensive task list
source_title: This One Command Makes Coding Agents Find All Their Mistakes (Use it Now)

claim: e2e testing with agents is token-heavy and time-consuming but saves tokens overall by catching issues early that would require costly agent iterations later
actionable: Accept longer e2e test runs and higher token usage as a legitimate cost of catching bugs upfront; batch e2e testing rather than running interactively
source_title: This One Command Makes Coding Agents Find All Their Mistakes (Use it Now)

claim: Include database query validation in e2e tests, not just UI checks—agents should verify that records created/updated/deleted in the UI are correctly reflected in the database
actionable: Build e2e tests that pair UI interactions with database queries to validate end-to-end data consistency
source_title: This One Command Makes Coding Agents Find All Their Mistakes (Use it Now)
